originalTitle: Fears for press freedom as billionaire takes control of East Africa's largest media house
title: Our Big News Kitchen Has a New Owner! Why We Hope Stories Stay True.
content:
Hello, My Amazing Africa News Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you a super interesting story from our wonderful continent, Africa! We're going to talk about something important that helps us learn about the world.

What is News, Anyway?

Have you ever wondered how you find out about things happening far away? Or even close to home? That's where news comes in! News is like a special window. It lets us peek out and see what's happening everywhere. It tells us about new inventions, brave people, exciting discoveries, and even important events. It helps us learn new things every day. News helps us understand our world better. It's like hearing a story about what happened when you weren't there. **Did you know?** The very first newspapers were written by hand a long, long time ago in ancient Rome!

Meet the News Helpers: Reporters!

Who makes all this news? Well, special people called reporters do! Think of them as super-smart detectives. They go out and look for facts. They ask lots of questions. They talk to people. They check if things are true. Their job is to find out what really happened. Reporters work very hard. They want to make sure the stories they tell are fair. They want to make sure the stories are true. They want to help everyone know what's going on.

The Big News Kitchen: A Media House

Now, where do these reporters work? They work in a special place called a "media house." Imagine a giant kitchen, but instead of cooking food, they are making stories! This media house is where they write newspaper articles, record radio shows, make TV programs, and create websites. It's a very busy place! Many people work there to bring you the news every day. They gather all the stories and put them together. Then they share them with you, so you can watch, listen, or read. **Did you know?** Africa has many amazing media houses that tell stories from all over our big continent!

A Super Rich Person Buys a Big News Kitchen!

So, here's the big news we want to talk about today! In a part of Africa called East Africa, there was a very, very big media house. It was the biggest one there! It told many, many stories to lots of people. But guess what? A super rich person, someone named Rostam Aziz, just bought it! This means he now owns this big news kitchen. It's like he bought the whole factory that makes our daily stories. This is a big change for that media house. It's a big change for all the reporters who work there. It's also a big change for all the people who read, watch, and listen to their news.

Who is a "Billionaire"?

Let's talk about that word, "billionaire." It's a very big word, for a very rich person! Imagine you have a few coins in your pocket. A billionaire has so much money, they could fill a whole swimming pool with coins! Or buy a thousand cars! Or even build a whole town! Rostam Aziz is a billionaire. He has lots and lots of money. So, when he bought the biggest media house in East Africa, it cost him a huge amount of money. **Did you know?** A billion is a 1 followed by nine zeros (1,000,000,000)! That's a lot of zeros!

Why Do Some People Worry?

Now, when a super rich person buys a big news kitchen, some people start to wonder. They have some questions. They worry about something called "press freedom" and "journalistic independence." These are big words, but let's make them simple. "Press freedom" means that the reporters should be FREE to tell ANY story they find. Even if the story talks about something powerful people did wrong. Or something that makes the owner of the news kitchen unhappy. Reporters should be free to find the truth and share it with everyone. "Journalistic independence" means that the reporters should tell stories FAIRLY. No one should tell them what to say. They should not take sides. They should just tell the honest truth, no matter what. So, some people are wondering if the news from this big media house will still be completely free. Will the reporters still be able to tell every story? Or will the new owner, Mr. Aziz, only want them to tell certain stories? Will they only say good things about him, or about his friends, or about things he likes? This is why some people are asking questions. They want to make sure the news stays fair and true for everyone.

Why is Telling the Truth So Important?

Why do we care so much about truth? Imagine you are playing a game with your friends. If someone doesn't tell the truth about the rules or the score, it's not fair, right? You wouldn't trust them anymore. It's the same with news. We need to trust the news to tell us the truth. When news tells us the truth, we can make good choices. We can learn about what is happening in our country and around the world. We can decide what we think is right or wrong. If the news is not independent, if someone tells it what to say, then it's like a biased referee in a game. It's not fair, and we can't trust it fully. We need news that helps us see the world clearly, not through someone else's special glasses. **Did you know?** In many countries, there are laws that try to protect press freedom because it's so important for people to know the truth!

What Happens Now?

So, what happens next for this big media house in East Africa? We don't know for sure yet. But people will be watching very carefully. They will be looking to see if the news stories stay fair and true. They will be hoping that the reporters can still do their important job freely. It's up to all of us to keep listening, watching, and reading the news. We can think about what we hear. We can ask questions. We can hope that the news will always show us the real picture of the world, just like a clear window.

Keep Asking Questions!

Learning about the world is an adventure! And knowing the truth is a superpower. So, keep your eyes and ears open. Keep asking "why?" and "how do you know?". That's how we all grow smarter and help make our world a better place.
summary: A very rich person has bought the biggest news company in East Africa, and many people hope the news will continue to tell true and fair stories for everyone.
whyMatters: This matters because news helps us learn about our world, and it's important that the news always tells us the honest truth without anyone telling them what to say.

originalTitle: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
title: A Special Talking Drum Comes Home to Ivory Coast!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have some super exciting news to share today! Guess what? A very, very special drum is finally coming home! It's travelling all the way back to its original home in a country called Ivory Coast, right here in Africa! This is big news because this drum is not just any drum. It's a truly special treasure, and it has been away from its home for a very, very long time. Let's find out more about this amazing drum!

A Very Special Drum

This drum is called a 'talking drum.' Isn't that a cool name? It's not magic, but it can make sounds that are a little bit like human speech! People who know how to play it can make it 'talk' by squeezing it under their arm and hitting it. This talking drum is also very 'sacred.' This means it's super important and very special to the people of Ivory Coast. It was used for important ceremonies and to tell important stories a long, long time ago. It could send messages, tell people about big events, or even call people together. Imagine sending a message across a village without a phone – that's what a talking drum could do! Did you know? Talking drums are so clever! Their sounds can change pitch, just like our voices when we ask a question or say something exciting! This helps them 'talk' and share messages over long distances.

Where Did the Drum Go?

Now, you might be wondering, if it's so special, why was it away from home? Well, my friends, many, many years ago, there was a time called 'colonial rule.' This was when some countries from Europe came and took control of many countries in Africa, including Ivory Coast. During those times, many beautiful and important things were taken from African countries. These things were often taken without asking, and they ended up in other countries, sometimes in museums. Our special talking drum was one of these things. It was taken from Ivory Coast by people from France. It has been kept in France for a very, very long time, far away from its true home and the people who cherished it. Did you know? Ivory Coast is a country in West Africa! It's famous for growing lots of cocoa beans, which are used to make yummy chocolate! Maybe your favourite chocolate bar comes from cocoa grown in Ivory Coast!

Why is it Coming Home Now?

This is the happy part of our story! The country of France has decided to give the talking drum back to Ivory Coast. They understand that this drum truly belongs to the people of Ivory Coast. It's like if your favourite toy went missing for a long time, and then someone found it and brought it back to you. You would be so happy, wouldn't you? That's how the people of Ivory Coast feel! It shows that countries are learning more about doing the right thing and giving back important items that were taken long ago. It's a really kind and respectful thing to do.

What Are "Cultural Artefacts"?

You might hear a big word like 'cultural artefacts.' Don't worry, it just means old things that people made a long, long time ago. These things tell us stories about their culture, their history, and how they lived. Think of them like old storybooks, but instead of words, they tell stories through their shape, their design, and what they were used for. The talking drum is a perfect example of a cultural artefact. Other cultural artefacts can be things like beautiful masks, special statues, old tools, or amazing artwork. Each one holds a piece of history and helps us understand our ancestors. Did you know? These old things are like clues from the past! They help historians and young people like you learn about what life was like many years ago in your country and all over Africa!

More Treasures Coming Home

This special drum coming home isn't the only good news! It's part of a bigger plan. Since 2017, France and other countries have been working to send back many other cultural artefacts to different African countries. So, it's not just one drum, but many wonderful treasures that are slowly finding their way back to their true homes across Africa. It's like a big family reunion for all these amazing old things! This means many countries in Africa will get back their important historical items. It helps them rebuild their collections of items that show their beautiful history and culture. Did you know? Many African countries have museums, just like other places in the world. These museums want to show their own history and culture to their people, especially the children!

Why Is This Important For Ivory Coast?

Getting this talking drum back is super important. It makes the people of Ivory Coast feel very happy and proud. It's like getting a piece of their family history back. The drum connects them to their ancestors, the people who lived there long, long ago. It helps the children of Ivory Coast learn about their past and understand their rich and beautiful culture. It reminds everyone that African history and culture are precious and important. It shows that the stories and traditions of Africa matter a lot. Did you know? Knowing your history is like knowing who you are! It helps you understand where you come from and makes you proud of your heritage!

A Big Welcome Home!

We can imagine the big welcome this drum will get when it finally arrives in Ivory Coast! There will probably be celebrations, music, and lots of happy faces. It will be looked after with great care and respect. This return shows how much people value and respect African culture and history. It's a wonderful step forward, bringing joy and pride to the people of Ivory Coast. So, let's celebrate this happy news together! A special talking drum is home, and it reminds us all about the power of history and culture! That's all the news for today, my amazing friends! Keep learning and stay curious! Bye for now!
summary: France has returned a very old and sacred talking drum to its rightful home in Ivory Coast, bringing an important piece of their history and culture back to the people.
whyMatters: This is important because getting back special old items like the talking drum helps the people of Ivory Coast learn about their ancestors and feel proud of their rich history and beautiful culture.

title: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
content: Partey is to appear at crown court next month, charged with raping a woman in December 2020.

content: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defence Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

title: Three more Iran football team members change minds over asylum
content: Four of seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

originalTitle: Record-breaker Dowman scores late as Arsenal beat Everton
title: Record-breaker Dowman scores late as Arsenal beat Everton
content: Sixteen-year-old Max Dowman becomes the youngest scorer in Premier League history, as Arsenal score twice in the last 10 minutes to beat Everton 2-0 and move 10 points clear at the top of the table.

originalTitle: Man City held by West Ham who move out of relegation zone
title: Man City held by West Ham who move out of relegation zone
content: West Ham come from behind to earn a point against Manchester City as Nuno Espirito Santo's side move out of the bottom three, while Pep Guardiola's side drop nine points behind leaders Arsenal in the Premier League.

originalTitle: Gordon goal gives Newcastle rare win at Chelsea
title: Gordon goal gives Newcastle rare win at Chelsea
content: Chelsea's hopes of qualifying for the Champions League are dealt a blow as Newcastle's Anthony Gordon scores the only goal of the game, to clinch a first away win at Stamford Bridge since 2012 for the Magpies.
